title: 1.1: Disable-alias integration test asserts the encoded unsubscribe GET response is successful (2xx/3xx)
1.2: Disable-alias integration test asserts the alias is persisted as disabled after the request
1.3: Disable-contact integration test asserts the encoded unsubscribe GET response is successful (2xx/3xx)
1.4: Disable-contact integration test asserts the contact is persisted as blocked after the request
1.5: Newsletter-unsubscribe integration test asserts the encoded unsubscribe GET response status_code is in the 200–399 range
1.6: Newsletter-unsubscribe integration test asserts the user notification preference is persisted as disabled
1.7: Original-unsubscribe forwarding test asserts the encoded unsubscribe GET response is successful (2xx/3xx)
1.8: Original-unsubscribe forwarding test asserts an outbound email is sent
1.9: Original-unsubscribe forwarding test asserts the outbound email is addressed to the original third-party recipient
2.1: Adds the encoded-unsubscribe integration tests to tests/handler/test_unsubscribe_handler.py
3.1: Tests are written as pytest-style functions (not unittest.TestCase classes)
3.2: New test functions are named with the test_ prefix
3.3: Integration tests use the flask_client fixture for HTTP requests
3.4: Assertions use Python assert statements (not self.assert* APIs)
4.1: Each required integration test authenticates a user session before calling the encoded unsubscribe endpoint
4.2: Each required integration test constructs the encoded_request value using the project unsubscribe encoding helper (e.g., UnsubscribeEncoder.encode_subject)
4.7: Original-unsubscribe forwarding test clears stored outbound emails before sending (e.g., mail_sender.purge_stored_emails())
